title: Course: COSC 243 - Computer Architecture
description: Introduction to computer organization and architecture. The computer is described as a hierarchy of levels, each performing well-defined functions. Comparisons are made in how levels and functions are implemented in various systems. Covers CPU organization and control, instruction set architecture, memory organizations, input/output structure, and parallel architectures.
prerequisites_text: COSC 241 completed with a grade of C or higher
content:
COSC 243 - Computer Architecture
Credits: 3
Prerequisites: COSC 241 completed with a grade of C or higher
Offered: Fall, Spring

Description: Introduction to computer organization and architecture. The computer is described as a hierarchy of levels, each performing well-defined functions. Comparisons are made in how levels and functions are implemented in various systems. Covers CPU organization and control, instruction set architecture, memory organizations, input/output structure, and parallel architectures.

Curriculum role:
  - BS Computer Science: Group A
  - BS Cloud Computing: Group A
  - Required for CS Minor
